title: 10 best herbal creams for swollen gums

remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Peppermint cream's antibacterial properties help to reduce the growth of bacteria that can contribute to gum inflammation and swelling, promoting a healthier oral environment.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in peppermint cream, such as menthol and methanol, help to reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with swollen gums, providing relief from discomfort.

Astringent properties: Peppermint cream's astringent properties help to constrict blood vessels and reduce bleeding, which can occur due to gum inflammation or injury, promoting healing and reducing the appearance of swelling.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita, also known as peppermint, creams helps with swollen gums because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The menthol in peppermint has a cooling effect on the gums, reducing swelling and pain. Additionally, peppermint's antiseptic qualities help combat bacterial infections that can cause gum inflammation. By soothing and calming the gums, peppermint creams provide relief from discomfort and promote a healthy oral environment, making it an effective natural remedy for swollen gums. Regular use can also aid in preventing future gum inflammation.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: Menthol has anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that help reduce swelling and ease pain in the gums.
Rosmarinic acid: Rosmarinic acid exhibits potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, which can help combat the underlying causes of gum inflammation and swelling.
Carvone: Carvone has been shown to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to combat bacterial infections and reduce inflammation in the gums.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high menthol and menthone content, which have anti-inflammatory properties.
Stems: Used as they contain oils that contribute to the anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects of the creams.
Roots: Used for their high concentration of menthol and other compounds that help reduce swelling and pain.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 ounces of fresh m x piperita leaves and flowers from your garden or a trusted supplier.
Steam-distill the m x piperita plant material for 2 hours to extract its essential oil.
Combine 2 ounces of the extracted m x piperita essential oil with 8 ounces of coconut oil and 2 ounces of beeswax.
Heat the mixture in a double boiler for 15 minutes until the beeswax is fully incorporated and the mixture is smooth.
Pour the cooled mixture into containers and store them in a cool dark place for 24 hours before use.

remedy_properties: Antimicrobial activity: Sage cream's antimicrobial properties help combat bacterial infections that can cause gum inflammation and swelling by killing or inhibiting the growth of harmful microorganisms.
Anti-inflammatory effects: Herbal sage contains compounds like rosmarinic acid, which have potent anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling, redness, and pain in affected gums.

Antioxidant activity: Sage's antioxidant properties help neutralize free radicals that can damage gum tissue and contribute to inflammation, promoting overall oral health and reducing the appearance of swollen gums.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Salvia officinalis, also known as sage, creams helps with swollen gums because of its anti-inflammatory properties and ability to reduce swelling and pain. The bioactive compounds in sage, such as rosmarinic acid and camphor, have antimicrobial and antioxidant effects that help combat gum infections and inflammation. Additionally, sage has been traditionally used to treat oral health issues due to its astringent and antiseptic properties, making it a natural remedy for swollen and irritated gums. Its soothing properties also promote healing and reduce discomfort.
remedy_constituents: Trigonelline: This alkaloid has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and alleviate pain in inflamed gums.

Rosmarinic acid: A phenolic compound, rosmarinic acid exhibits potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, which can help alleviate swollen gums by reducing inflammation and promoting healing.
Carnosic acid: A phenolic diterpene, carnosic acid possesses strong antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling, ease pain, and promote healthy gum tissue.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and antimicrobial properties, which help to reduce inflammation and prevent infection.
Leaves: Contain compounds like rosmarinic acid and caffeic acid, which have anti-inflammatory and astringent properties to soothe swollen gums.
Leaves: Their antiseptic and antibacterial properties help to prevent infection and promote healing in swollen gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30 grams of fresh salvia officinalis leaves and flowers at peak potency for maximum benefits.
Steep the herb in 200 milliliters of boiling water for 10 to 15 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean bowl to separate plant material.
Mix 20 grams of beeswax with 50 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler at 160 to 180 degrees fahrenheit for 10 to 15 minutes.
Combine the infused liquid with 20 grams of shea butter and 10 milliliters of vitamin e oil in the cooled mixture.

remedy_properties: Inflammation reduction: The anti-inflammatory compounds present in Kansas coneflower creams, such as triterpenoids and sesquiterpenes, help reduce swelling and pain in the gums by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Antibacterial properties: The cream's antibacterial activity helps to combat oral bacteria that can cause gum inflammation and infection, promoting a healthy balance of oral flora and reducing the risk of complications.

Wound healing acceleration: The wound-healing compounds in Kansas coneflower creams, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, accelerate the repair process by improving blood flow, collagen synthesis, and tissue regeneration, which helps to reduce gum swelling and promote a faster recovery.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Echinacea angustifolia, also known as kansas coneflower, creams helps with swollen gums because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The active compounds present in the plant, such as alkylamides and caffeic acid, have been shown to reduce swelling and prevent bacterial growth, thereby alleviating gum inflammation. By applying the cream directly to the affected area, individuals can experience relief from swollen gums, promoting overall oral health and well-being. This natural remedy can be a valuable addition to any gum care routine.
remedy_constituents: Alkylamides: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with gum inflammation.
Cichoric acid: As a phenolic compound, cichoric acid exhibits antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help combat bacterial infections and reduce gum inflammation.
Iridoid glycosides: Iridoid glycosides have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help soothe and calm inflamed gums, reducing swelling and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain a higher concentration of alkaloids, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in gums.
Leaves: They are rich in flavonoids and phenolic compounds that possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can aid in soothing and calming swollen gums.

Roots: (Also mentioned first because roots are more commonly used) They also contain a higher concentration of polysaccharides, which are believed to stimulate the immune system and promote healing of gum tissues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of dried echinacea angustifolia roots in late summer or early fall.
Extract 10 grams of the dried roots using 500ml of carrier oil in a 3-week period.
Steep the oil and plant mixture at 60 degrees celsius for 2 hours daily.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth and discard the solids after 2 weeks of steeping.
Mix 100 grams of beeswax with 50 grams of coconut oil and 20 grams of the extracted echinacea oil.

remedy_properties: Antioxidant activity: Pot marigold cream's antioxidant properties help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the gums, thereby alleviating swelling.
Anti-inflammatory compounds: The cream contains flavonoids and terpenes, which have anti-inflammatory effects that help reduce gum swelling, pain, and redness.

Wound-healing and soothing properties: Pot marigold's mucilages and polysaccharides in the cream create a protective barrier that soothes and calms irritated gums, promoting wound healing and reducing discomfort.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Calendula officinalis, also known as pot marigold, creams helps with swollen gums because of its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The cream soothes and calms the gums, reducing redness and swelling. Its natural astringent properties also help to reduce bleeding and promote healing. Additionally, Calendula's antimicrobial properties help to prevent infections, promoting a healthy environment for the gums to recover. This makes it a popular natural remedy for gum inflammation and swelling, providing relief and promoting overall oral health.
remedy_constituents: Calendulin: Helps with swollen gums by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines, which reduces inflammation and promotes healing.
Quercetin: Helps with swollen gums by acting as a potent anti-inflammatory agent, reducing swelling and pain by blocking the action of inflammatory enzymes.
Kaempferol: Helps with swollen gums by exhibiting antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce oxidative stress and inflammation in the affected area.
remedy_parts: Flowers: These are the most commonly used parts due to their high concentration of anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds, such as triterpene saponins and flavonoids.
Leaves: Leaves are also used in calendula creams because they contain a variety of bioactive compounds that help to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing.
Stems: Stems are another part used in calendula creams, as they contain similar bioactive compounds to flowers and leaves, which help to soothe and calm swollen gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 flowers of calendula officinalis in full bloom for a sufficient quantity of petals.
Dry the harvested flowers in a low-temperature oven at 150°f for 2 hours.
Steep 2 tablespoons of dried calendula petals in 2 cups of carrier oil such as coconut oil for 2 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth and discard the solids after 2 weeks of steeping.
Mix 1 tablespoon of the infused oil with 1 tablespoon of beeswax and 1 tablespoon of distilled water to create a smooth cream.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Stevia cream contains anti-inflammatory compounds such as flavonoids, which help to reduce swelling and pain in the gums by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins, leading to a decrease in inflammation.

Antioxidants: The antioxidants present in stevia cream, including polyphenols and saponins, help to neutralize free radicals that can contribute to gum inflammation and damage, promoting overall oral health.

Wound-healing properties: Stevia has been shown to have wound-healing properties, which can aid in the repair of damaged gum tissue and promote faster healing, reducing the appearance of swollen gums.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stevia rebaudiana, also known as stevia, creams helps with swollen gums because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The natural compounds present in Stevia rebaudiana have been shown to reduce swelling and ease pain in gum tissue. The cream's soothing effects also help to calm irritated gums and promote healing, reducing the risk of infection. Additionally, Stevia rebaudiana's antibacterial properties prevent the growth of bacteria that can cause gum inflammation, leading to healthier and more comfortable gums.
remedy_constituents: Sterols: Sterols, specifically stigmasterol and beta-sitosterol, have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in gums.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids like kaempferol and quercetin in Stevia rebaudiana exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities, which can help alleviate swollen gums by reducing inflammation and oxidative stress.

Triterpenoids: Triterpenoids, such as ursolic acid and oleanolic acid, have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help combat swollen gums by reducing inflammation and preventing infection.
remedy_parts: Leaves: These are the primary source of steviosides, which are used to make creams for swollen gums due to their anti-inflammatory properties.
Roots: Steviosides found in the roots also have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling in the gums.
Stems: The stems of Stevia rebaudiana also contain steviosides, which can be used to make creams for swollen gums due to their analgesic and anti-inflammatory effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest stevia rebaudiana leaves by hand when they are fully grown and have reached their optimal sweetness level.
Dry the stevia rebaudiana leaves in a warm and well-ventilated area for 7 to 10 days.
Infuse the dried stevia rebaudiana leaves in a carrier oil such as coconut oil for 2 to 3 weeks.
Strain the infused oil and discard the solids by using a cheesecloth or a fine mesh filter.
Whip the infused oil with a natural emulsifier and a sweetener such as stevia powder to create a smooth and even consistency.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as aloin and aloe-emodin, help reduce swelling and pain in swollen gums by decreasing inflammation and alleviating discomfort.

Antimicrobial property: The antimicrobial properties of aloe vera, including its ability to inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi, help combat gum infections that can cause swelling and promote healing of damaged tissues.

Wound-healing property: Aloe vera's natural soothing and moisturizing properties accelerate wound healing by promoting collagen synthesis, improving tissue repair, and enhancing the regrowth of healthy gum tissue.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe vera, also known as aloe, creams helps with swollen gums because of its anti-inflammatory and soothing properties. The gel extracted from the plant contains compounds like aloin and aloe-emodin, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and swelling. Aloe vera creams can help to calm irritated gums, reduce redness and discomfort, and promote healing. Additionally, its antibacterial properties can help to prevent infections and promote a healthy oral environment, making it an effective remedy for swollen gums. This natural remedy can bring relief and comfort to those experiencing gum inflammation.
remedy_constituents: Aloe-emodin: Aloe-emodin is an anthraquinone compound that has anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and pain in gums.
Salicylic acid: Salicylic acid is a phenolic compound that has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, which help combat gum inflammation and infection.

Vitamins and minerals (especially Vitamin C and Manganese): The vitamins and minerals present in Aloe vera creams, especially Vitamin C and Manganese, have antioxidant properties that help reduce inflammation and promote wound healing in gums.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are the primary source of Aloe vera gel, which is used in creams to reduce inflammation and soothe swollen gums due to its anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: The juice extracted from Aloe vera leaves also contains antibacterial properties that help combat gum infections.
Leaves: The gel from Aloe vera leaves is rich in vitamins and minerals, which aid in promoting healthy gum tissue and reducing swelling.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 100g of aloe vera gel and 50g of beeswax to prepare the base mixture.
Melt the beeswax in a double boiler over medium heat for about 10 minutes stirring occasionally.
Combine 30g of shea butter and 20g of coconut oil with the melted beeswax mixture.
Add 10g of vitamin e oil and 10g of essential oil to the mixture for extra nourishment.
Pour the final mixture into a glass jar and let it cool and set for 30 minutes.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Herbal teas like chamomile, calendula, and peppermint have anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce swelling and alleviate pain in swollen gums.
Antimicrobial properties: Herbs like sage, echinacea, and goldenseal contain antimicrobial agents that combat bacteria and viruses causing gum infections, promoting healing and reducing inflammation.

Astringent and soothing properties: Herbal teas like tea tree, licorice root, and marshmallow root have astringent and soothing effects that help to reduce swelling, ease pain, and protect the gums from further irritation.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Camellia sinensis, also known as tea, creams helps with swollen gums because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The camellia oil extracted from Camellia sinensis leaves has been traditionally used to soothe and calm irritated tissues, reducing inflammation and promoting healing. When applied topically, the cream can help to reduce swelling and alleviate pain in the gums, making it a natural and effective remedy for gum inflammation and other oral health issues. Its natural antibacterial properties also help prevent infection.
remedy_constituents: Theaflavins: These flavonoids have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and inflammation in the gums by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.
Catechins: These polyphenolic compounds have potent antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects that help combat gum infections and reduce inflammation, thereby alleviating swollen gums.
Gallic acid: This phenolic acid has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that help reduce the severity of gum inflammation, prevent infection, and promote healing of swollen gums.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used to make creams for swollen gums due to their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties, which help reduce swelling and pain.
Stems: They are used to make creams for swollen gums because they contain flavonoids and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects.
Buds: They are used to make creams for swollen gums due to their astringent and anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Infuse 2 teaspoons of dried camellia sinensis leaves in 2 cups of boiling water for 5-7 minutes.
Steep the herbal infusion in a warm place for 4 hours to allow it to cool and reduce.
Strain the cooled infusion through a cheesecloth into a clean bowl to remove the solids.
Mix 1/2 cup of a carrier oil such as coconut oil with the strained infusion to create the base.
Whip the mixture with an electric mixer until it thickens into a creamy consistency taking about 5 minutes.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Bigleaf hydrangea contains flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids that have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce swelling and discomfort in the gums by inhibiting the production of prostaglandins, a type of inflammatory mediator.

Antibacterial properties: The cream may contain antibacterial compounds like salicin, which can help combat bacterial infections that contribute to gum inflammation and disease by disrupting the cell membranes of bacteria and preventing their growth.

Antioxidant activity: Bigleaf hydrangea's antioxidant properties can help protect the gums from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, reducing the risk of chronic inflammation and promoting healthy tissue repair.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hydrangea macrophylla, also known as bigleaf hydrangea, creams helps with swollen gums because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The root of the plant contains compounds that have been shown to reduce swelling and alleviate pain. The creams are applied topically, allowing the active ingredients to penetrate directly into the affected area. This targeted approach helps to reduce inflammation and promote healing, making it easier to manage swollen gums and maintain good oral health. Regular use can lead to improved gum health and overall well-being.
remedy_constituents: Quercetin: Quercetin is a flavonoid that has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and pain in the gums.
Kaempferol: Kaempferol is a flavonol that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which can help alleviate swollen gums and promote healing.
Tannins: Tannins are a type of polyphenol that can help reduce inflammation and promote astringent effects, which can help constrict swollen tissues in the gums.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for their anti-inflammatory and astringent properties.
Roots: Used for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties.
Flowers: Used for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1 cup of fresh hydrangea macrophylla flowers in the early morning to preserve their potency and fragrance.
Steep the flowers in 2 cups of hot distilled water for 10 minutes to release their bioactive compounds.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth to separate the liquid from the solids and discard the solids.
Combine the liquid with 1/2 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of coconut oil in a double boiler to melt the mixture.
Whip the mixture until it thickens and cools to create a smooth and creamy texture for application.

remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory properties, which helps to reduce swelling and inflammation in the gums by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.
Licoricidin: This bioactive compound exhibits antimicrobial activity, which can help to combat bacterial infections that contribute to gum disease and swelling, while also promoting wound healing.

Flavonoids: These polyphenolic compounds have antioxidant properties, which help to protect the gums from further damage caused by free radicals, thereby reducing inflammation and promoting overall oral health.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra, also known as licorice, creams helps with swollen gums because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The active compound, glycyrrhizin, has been shown to inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes, which contribute to swelling and pain. By reducing inflammation, licorice creams create a soothing environment for the gums, promoting healing and relaxation. This natural remedy provides effective relief for gum inflammation.

Liquiritin apioside: This flavonoid glycoside has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help combat bacterial infections that may contribute to gum inflammation and swelling.

remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in glycyrrhizic acid, which has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in gums.
Leaves: Contain flavonoids and phenolic compounds that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help soothe and heal swollen gums.
Stems: Contain glycosides and saponins that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help reduce swelling and promote healing in gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 500 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots from a trusted supplier to ensure quality.
Infuse 200 grams of the dried roots in 1 liter of boiling water for 10 minutes.
Strain the infused liquid through a cheesecloth and discard the solids after 30 minutes.
Mix 200 grams of beeswax and 100 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler at 180 degrees fahrenheit for 20 minutes.
Combine the infused liquid and the beeswax mixture in a heat-proof bowl and stir until cooled.

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Tasmanian Blue Gum (Eucalyptus globulus) has been shown to reduce inflammation and swelling in the gums due to its ability to inhibit pro-inflammatory enzymes.
Antimicrobial properties: The cream's antimicrobial properties help combat bacterial and fungal infections that can cause swollen gums, reducing the risk of further infection and promoting healing.
Antioxidant properties: Tasmanian Blue Gum's antioxidant compounds help protect gum tissue from oxidative damage caused by free radicals, which can contribute to gum inflammation and swelling.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus, also known as Tasmanian blue gum, creams helps with swollen gums because of its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The active compounds in Eucalyptus globulus, such as eucalyptol, have been shown to reduce swelling and ease pain in the gums. Additionally, its natural antiseptic properties help to combat bacterial infections that can cause gum inflammation, making it an effective remedy for swollen gums. Regular application of Eucalyptus globulus cream can provide relief and promote healthy gum tissue.
remedy_constituents: Cineole (Eucalyptol): This terpene helps to reduce inflammation and relieve pain in swollen gums by acting as a local anesthetic and anti-inflammatory agent.

Phenolic acids (Catechins and Gallic acid): These compounds exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling, prevent infection, and promote healing in inflamed gums.

Flavonoids (Quercetin and Kaempferol): These plant-derived compounds have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to reduce swelling, prevent infection, and promote healing in inflamed gums.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in eucalyptol, a natural antiseptic and anti-inflammatory compound that helps to reduce swelling and relieve pain in swollen gums.
Barks: Contain salicylic acid, a natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory agent that helps to reduce pain and inflammation in swollen gums.
Stems: Contain flavonoids and phenolic compounds that have antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, helping to prevent infection and reduce inflammation in swollen gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 fresh eucalyptus globulus leaves for their essential oil content and store them in a cool dry place.
Steam distill the leaves to extract their essential oil in a 2:1 water to leaves ratio for 2 hours.
Mix 250ml of distilled water with 50g of beeswax and 50g of shea butter in a double boiler.
Add 10ml of the eucalyptus globulus essential oil to the melted mixture and stir well for 5 minutes.
Pour the mixture into a clean glass container and let it cool and solidify at room temperature for 30 minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion creams helps with swollen gums because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which target the root cause of gum swelling. The cream's active ingredients, such as taraxasterol and ursolic acid, work to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with swollen gums. Additionally, dandelion's natural antiseptic properties help combat bacterial infections that can contribute to gum swelling. By soothing and calming the affected area, dandelion creams provide effective relief for those suffering from gum inflammation and discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Dandelion contains compounds like taraxasterol, which has been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate pain, making it an effective remedy for reducing swelling in the gums.

Antimicrobial: The antibacterial and antifungal properties of dandelion can help combat oral infections that may be contributing to swollen gums, promoting a healthy mouth environment and preventing further infection.

Healing and soothing: Dandelion's high mucilage content provides a protective coating for the mucous membranes in the mouth, helping to soothe and calm irritated tissue, reducing discomfort and inflammation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, making it effective in reducing swelling and pain associated with gum inflammation.
Buds: Containing bioactive flavonoids and polyphenols, which help to reduce inflammation and promote healing of the affected area.
remedy_recipe: Gather dried taraxacum officinale leaves and stems in sufficient quantity for the desired amount of cream.
Decoct dried taraxacum officinale ingredients in boiling water for 30 minutes then strain the liquid.
Combine strained taraxacum officinale infusion with beeswax and coconut oil in a double boiler to melt the wax at 160°f.
Whip the cooled and solidified cream mixture into a smooth consistency using an electric mixer or whisk.
Pour the prepared taraxacum officinale cream into sterilized containers and allow it to set for at least 2 hours before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St John's Wort creams helps with swollen gums because they contain a unique combination of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and volatile oils that work together to reduce inflammation and alleviate discomfort. The anti-inflammatory properties of these compounds help to constrict blood vessels, which decreases swelling and bleeding in the gums. Additionally, St John's Wort creams may also stimulate the healing process by increasing collagen synthesis, promoting tissue repair, and reducing bacterial growth.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: St. John's Wort contains antioxidants that help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which can contribute to swelling and discomfort in the gums.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids and terpenes present in St. John's Wort cream have anti-inflammatory effects, which can help reduce redness, swelling, and pain associated with gum inflammation.

Antibacterial properties: St. John's Wort has been shown to exhibit antibacterial activity against certain oral bacteria, such as Porphyromonas gingivalis, which is a common cause of periodontal disease and swollen gums.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of St John's Wort contain a high concentration of flavonoids and hyperforin, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate swollen gums.
Leaves: The leaves of St John's Wort are rich in tannins, which provide astringent and anti-inflammatory effects when used to soothe and calm swollen gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh hypericum perforatum leaves and flowers and dry them for at least four hours in a cool dark place.
Grind one part dried hypericum perforatum into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or blender.
Mix the powdered herb with two parts coconut oil and one part beeswax in a small saucepan over low heat.
Heat the mixture for about 10 minutes stirring occasionally until the beeswax is fully melted and the mixture is smooth.
Strain the cream through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass jar and let it cool completely at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Damask rose creams helps with swollen gums because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. The creams contain salicylic acid, a derivative of aspirin that is naturally present in roses, which effectively reduces swelling and alleviates pain. Additionally, the cream's soothing and calming effects help to reduce redness and discomfort associated with swollen gums. The gentle and non-irritating nature of damask rose creams makes it an effective and comfortable solution for those seeking natural relief from gum inflammation.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Damask rose contains compounds like phenolic acids, flavonoids, and terpenes that have antibacterial activity, which helps to combat bacterial infections that may be causing gum swelling.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as beta-carotene and vitamin E, reduce inflammation and alleviate pain in swollen gums, promoting a healthy oral environment.

Wound-healing properties: Damask rose's natural antioxidants and flavonoids accelerate wound healing by improving blood circulation, reducing swelling, and enhancing collagen synthesis, which helps to soothe and repair damaged gum tissue.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the damask rose plant contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and pain in swollen gums.
Fruits: Damask rose fruits, also known as hips, are rich in antioxidants and vitamins A and C, which promote gum health and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh rosa damascena flowers and dry them in the shade for at least 24 hours.
Combine 20g of dried rosa damascena flowers with 500ml of hot distilled water and steep for 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container and discard solids.
Mix 20g of beeswax with 100g of sweet almond oil in a double boiler and heat until melted and smooth.
Combine the herbal infusion with the melted wax-oil mixture, stirring well to emulsify, and pour into sterilized jars for storage at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender creams helps with swollen gums because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The cream's soothing essence, derived from the calming herb Lavandula angustifolia, penetrates deep into the gum tissue to reduce swelling and alleviate discomfort. As it cools and calms the affected area, it also promotes healthy blood flow, which aids in the healing process. Additionally, lavender's antiseptic properties help combat bacterial buildup that can contribute to swollen gums, providing a natural solution for soothing and supporting oral health.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial property: Lavender's antibacterial properties help to reduce the growth of bacteria in the mouth, which can contribute to swollen gums by combating infections and inflammation.
Anti-inflammatory property: The anti-inflammatory compounds in lavender cream, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, help to reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with gum inflammation.
Wound-healing property: Lavender's ability to accelerate wound healing can aid in the recovery of swollen gums by promoting tissue repair and regeneration, reducing the risk of further infection.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in calcium and magnesium, lavender roots help to reduce inflammation and ease pain associated with swollen gums.
Flowers: The antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties of lavender flowers help to combat bacterial infections and soothe the discomfort caused by swollen gums.
remedy_recipe: Infuse dried lavandula angustifolia flowers in boiling water for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bowl and discard solids immediately.
Mix 100g of beeswax pellets with 50ml of the lavender infusion and heat until melted at 70°c to 80°c.
Combine 20ml of coconut oil and 10ml of sweet almond oil and stir until well mixed with the warm lavender-infused beeswax mixture.
Pour the mixture into sterilized glass jars and let cool for at least 30 minutes before sealing.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Spearmint creams helps with swollen gums because they contain natural anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce swelling and ease discomfort. The menthol in spearmint relaxes the gum tissue, allowing for improved blood flow and reducing redness. Additionally, the antimicrobial properties of spearmint help to combat bacterial buildup that can contribute to gum inflammation. By soothing and calming the gums, spearmint creams provide quick relief from swelling and discomfort, making them a natural and effective solution for maintaining healthy, happy teeth and gums.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Spearmint cream contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids, which help reduce swelling and inflammation in the gums, providing relief from pain and discomfort.

Antibacterial properties: The antimicrobial agents present in spearmint cream help combat bacterial infections that can cause gum disease, reducing the severity of swollen gums and promoting a healthy oral environment.

Wound-healing properties: Spearmint's wound-healing properties, attributed to its high levels of antioxidants and vitamins A and C, aid in the recovery process by promoting collagen production, tissue repair, and reduced scar formation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the spearmint plant have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with gum inflammation.
Barks: The barks of the spearmint plant contain flavonoids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, making them effective in soothing swollen gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh mint leaves of mentha spicata and rinse them thoroughly with distilled water.
Combine 20 grams of dried mint leaves with 100 milliliters of carrier oil in a glass jar.
Allow the mixture to steep for 24 hours at room temperature, then strain it through a cheesecloth into another container.
Mix 50 grams of beeswax with 30 grams of coconut oil and heat them together until melted and well combined.
Add the infused mint extract to the melted wax-oil mixture and stir well before pouring it into tin tubes for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow creams helps with swollen gums because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's active compounds, including acetic acid and sesquiterpenes, work to reduce swelling and alleviate pain associated with gum inflammation. Additionally, yarrow contains antioxidants that help combat free radicals and promote healing in the affected area. As a result, using herbal yarrow creams can provide effective relief from swollen gums, promoting healthy oral tissues and a reduced risk of further complications.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Yarrow cream contains anti-inflammatory compounds like chromonins and flavonoids, which help reduce swelling and alleviate pain in swollen gums.

Antimicrobial properties: The antimicrobial properties of yarrow cream, including its ability to inhibit the growth of bacteria and fungi, help prevent infections that can cause gum swelling and promote healing.
Astringent properties: Yarrow's astringent properties help reduce bleeding and inflammation by constricting blood vessels and reducing excess mucus production in the gums.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in sesquiterpene lactones, which have anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that help to reduce swelling and ease pain associated with gum inflammation.
Buds: Yarrow buds contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that exhibit antimicrobial activity, making them effective against bacteria that cause periodontal disease and promoting healthy gums.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh achillea millefolium leaves and dry them in a cool dark place for at least 24 hours or use dried leaves that have been stored properly.
Combine 20 grams of dried achillea millefolium leaves with 100 milliliters of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 14 days, shaking the jar twice daily to allow for optimal extraction.
Strain the oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another container, discarding the solids and retaining the infused oil.
Mix the infused oil with 20 grams of beeswax and 10 milliliters of vitamin e oil in a double boiler, heating until melted and smooth to create the final cream.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel creams helps with swollen gums because they contain flavonoids and terpenes, which possess potent anti-inflammatory properties. When applied topically to the affected area, these compounds work to reduce swelling, alleviate pain, and combat infection. Fennel's natural antibacterial agents also help eliminate bacteria that can contribute to gum inflammation. By reducing inflammation and combating bacterial growth, fennel creams provide a natural solution for soothing swollen gums and promoting overall oral health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Fennel creams contain anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids, terpenes, and lignans that help reduce swelling and inflammation in the gums, providing relief from discomfort and pain.

Antimicrobial properties: Fennel's antimicrobial properties help combat bacteria and other microorganisms that can cause gum infections and inflammation, promoting a healthy oral environment and reducing the risk of further complications.

Digestive soothing compounds: Fennel creams often contain digestive soothing compounds like mucilage, which helps to reduce inflammation and discomfort in the mouth and throat, while also aiding digestion and alleviating symptoms like bloating and indigestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: Fennel roots have anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective in reducing swelling and pain associated with gum inflammation.
Seeds: The seeds of the fennel plant contain compounds like anethole, which has natural antibacterial and antifungal properties, helping to combat gum infections that cause swelling.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 tablespoons of dried foeniculum vulgare leaves and grind them into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle within 30 minutes.
Combine the powdered foeniculum vulgare with 6 tablespoons of coconut oil and 2 tablespoons of beeswax in a double boiler, heating the mixture for 10-15 minutes until melted.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool to around 160°f, then add 4 drops of peppermint essential oil and mix well for 30 seconds.
Pour the mixture into sterilized glass jars and refrigerate at a temperature below 40°f for at least 2 hours or overnight to solidify the cream.
Once solidified, use the foeniculum vulgare creams as needed, applying a small amount topically to affected areas of gums two to three times daily for optimal results within 7-10 days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass creams helps with swollen gums because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and ease discomfort. The antiseptic and astringent qualities of lemongrass also help to combat bacterial infections that can cause gum inflammation. Additionally, the soothing and calming effects of lemongrass cream provide relief from pain and discomfort, allowing for improved oral hygiene and reduced risk of further infection. This natural remedy provides a gentle yet effective solution for swollen gums, promoting healthy and comfortable teeth and gums.
remedy_properties: Antibacterial properties: Lemongrass contains compounds like citral and geraniol, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria such as Streptococcus mutans, a common cause of gum infections.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The flavonoids present in lemongrass, including kaempferol and quercetin, can help reduce inflammation and alleviate pain associated with swollen gums by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory enzymes.

Antioxidant properties: Lemongrass contains antioxidants like vitamin C and beta-carotene, which help protect against oxidative stress and cell damage in the mouth, promoting healthy gum tissue and reducing the severity of swelling.
remedy_parts: Roots: Lemongrass roots have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and pain in the gums.
Rhyzomes: The thick, underground stems (rhyzomes) of lemongrass contain antioxidants and flavonoids that soothe gum inflammation and promote healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 50g of dried cymbopogon citratus leaves and grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 2 minutes.
Combine the powdered herb with 100ml of distilled water in a heat-resistant glass bowl and steep for 10 minutes at room temperature.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container, squeezing out as much liquid as possible from the solids.
Mix the herbal extract with 200g of petroleum jelly and 20ml of coconut oil in a double boiler until fully incorporated and smooth for 10 minutes.
Pour the cream mixture into sterilized glass jars, seal tightly, and store in the refrigerator at a consistent temperature below 40°f for up to 6 months before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sweet marjoram creams helps with swollen gums because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties. Marjoram contains compounds like cineole, borneol, and beta-pinene that work together to reduce swelling and ease pain in the gums. These natural ingredients also have antimicrobial properties, which help combat gum infections and prevent further inflammation. When applied topically as a cream, marjoram's soothing properties can quickly alleviate discomfort and promote healing, providing relief from swollen gums.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory property: Marjoram's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as terpenes and flavonoids, help reduce swelling and alleviate pain in inflamed gums by decreasing prostaglandins production.
Antimicrobial property: Marjoram's antimicrobial activity helps combat bacteria, viruses, and fungi that can cause gum infections, reducing the risk of further inflammation and promoting healing.

Analgesic and antispasmodic property: Marjoram's analgesic and antispasmodic properties help relieve pain, discomfort, and stiffness in the gums, making it easier to eat, speak, and perform daily activities.
remedy_parts: Roots: Sweet marjoram roots contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce swelling and alleviate pain in swollen gums.
Flowers: Marjoram flowers have antibacterial properties, which can prevent gum infections and promote healthy gum tissue.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh origanum majorana leaves and measure out 2 tablespoons for the cream preparation.
Combine the harvested leaves with 1 cup of distilled water in a heat-resistant glass bowl and steep for 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass measuring cup, discarding solids after 5 minutes.
Mix 1 tablespoon of beeswax pellets with 2 tablespoons of coconut oil in a small saucepan over low heat for 5 minutes.
Combine the strained plant infusion with the beeswax-coconut oil mixture and whip until smooth, allowing to cool at room temperature.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for swollen gums are topical remedies containing plant extracts that help reduce inflammation and soothe swollen gums. These creams typically include ingredients such as aloe vera, tea tree oil, and eucalyptus, which have antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties. Examples include creams containing turmeric, ginger, and myrrh. By alleviating discomfort and promoting healing, these creams improve the quality of life for individuals suffering from gum inflammation, allowing them to eat, speak, and perform daily activities with greater ease and comfort.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to swollen gums that are treated with herbal creams are various skin issues, including eczema, acne, and rosacea. Herbal creams containing ingredients like tea tree oil, aloe vera, and chamomile can help soothe and calm inflamed skin, reducing redness and discomfort. These natural remedies may also be used to treat minor wounds, burns, and sunburns, as well as conditions like psoriasis and dermatitis.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal creams that help with swollen gums is a blend of Aloe Vera, Echinacea, and Calendula. Aloe Vera soothes and calms inflammation, while Echinacea boosts the immune system to fight off underlying infections. Calendula promotes wound healing and reduces inflammation, making it an effective remedy for swollen gums. Apply these creams individually or combine them to create a potent paste, and gently massage onto affected areas for relief from pain and discomfort, promoting healthy gum recovery.
